sales-tax.customIntro: Tennessee has one of the highest combined sales tax rates in the United States — a 7% state rate on most goods (with a higher 9.75% on food and food ingredients from grocery stores being replaced by a reduced rate) plus local additions that push most Tennessee shoppers to a combined rate of 9.25% to 9.75%.
sales-tax.formula: Price × 0.0925 = sales tax owed (Nashville / Davidson County example)

taxFreeWeekends.name: Back-to-School Sales Tax Holiday
taxFreeWeekends.items: Clothing $100 or less per item, school supplies $100 or less per item, computers $1,500 or less

taxFreeWeekends.name: Firearms Sales Tax Holiday
taxFreeWeekends.items: Handguns, long guns, and ammunition — no price cap

faqs.question: What is the sales tax rate in Tennessee?
faqs.answer: Tennessee's state sales tax rate is 7% on most goods and 4% on food and food ingredients. Local counties add between 1.5% and 2.75%, bringing most combined rates to 9.25%–9.75%. Tennessee consistently ranks among the top 3 states for highest combined sales tax rates in the US.

faqs.question: What is the sales tax rate in Nashville, Tennessee?
faqs.answer: Nashville is in Davidson County, which has a combined sales tax rate of 9.25% — the 7% state rate plus a 2.25% Davidson County local rate. This applies to most retail purchases within Nashville and the surrounding Metro area.

faqs.question: Does Tennessee have a sales tax holiday in 2026?
faqs.answer: Yes. Tennessee holds an annual back-to-school sales tax holiday on the last Friday through Sunday of July. Qualifying items include clothing at $100 or less per item, school supplies at $100 or less, and computers at $1,500 or less. Tennessee also holds a firearms holiday in September.

faqs.question: Is there sales tax on groceries in Tennessee?
faqs.answer: Yes, but at a reduced rate. Tennessee taxes food and food ingredients at 4% at the state level instead of the standard 7%. Local taxes still apply on top, so the effective rate on groceries is typically around 5.5%–6.75% depending on the county — still one of the higher grocery tax rates nationally.

faqs.question: What is the sales tax in Memphis, Tennessee?
faqs.answer: Memphis is in Shelby County, which has the highest combined sales tax rate in Tennessee at 9.75% — the 7% state rate plus a 2.75% Shelby County addition. This applies to most retail purchases made within Memphis city limits.

faqs.question: Do you pay sales tax on a used car in Tennessee?
faqs.answer: Yes. Tennessee charges sales tax on used vehicle purchases at the combined local rate. The standard 7% state rate applies plus the applicable county local rate, typically resulting in a 9.25%–9.75% total tax on the purchase price.
